name: MCP News Feed
description: News about MCP, AI agents, MCP servers, tool integrations, security, developer tooling, and enterprise AI adoption.

articles.title: AI Agents as Digital Employees: Architecture and Lessons from Practice
articles.summary: The "digital employee" is the most heavily sold and least understood product of 2026. Vendor slides promise a colleague who never sleeps. articles.title: MCP authentication in 2026: how OAuth flipped the server's role (and why every agent differs)
articles.summary: A couple of years ago we all collided with a brand-new technology that grew up alongside agents and LLMs: MCP. articles.title: I pointed my routing tester at a server I didn't build. One tool was quietly stealing two others' jobs.
articles.summary: A few weeks ago I built a small tool called routeproof. It tests one narrow, annoying thing about MCP servers: when an AI host decides which of your tools to call, the only thing its model sees is each tool's name, description, and input schema. Not your code. If two descriptions overlap, or one is...

articles.title: Announcing the Monetization Gateway: charge for any resource behind Cloudflare via x402
articles.summary: We're opening the waitlist for our Monetization Gateway, which will allow you to charge for any web page, dataset, API, or MCP tool behind Cloudflare. The charges will settle in stablecoins over the x402 open protocol, with no payments stack of your own to build.

articles.title: Enterprise-Managed Authorization: Zero-touch OAuth for MCP
articles.summary: The Enterprise-Managed Authorization extension to the Model Context Protocol is now stable, enabling organizations to centrally provision MCP server access through their identity provider so users get connected servers on first login without per-app OAuth.
